compiled_state.author: Iso Tester

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body:
Plan-review PASS (signed, 0 blocking, 10 advisory + 4 overflow). Triage + design decisions:
- [E2 f9e4b/ff3ab/fea34 field name + semantics] DECIDED: canonical field = applies_when, a list[str] of trigger TAGS (declarative data, JSON-serializable for project move files — NOT a callable). A move is APPLICABLE iff applies_when is absent/empty OR contains 'always' OR intersects the active_triggers set passed at selection time. 'triggers' in prose = the active_triggers argument; the registry FIELD is applies_when. Existing plan-review moves declare NO applies_when => always-applicable => no behavior change.
- [E2 fafb1 selection semantics] DECIDED + ordered: (1) gate-on-surviving>0 (0 => no coach call, []); (2) deterministic applicability filter (applies_when vs active_triggers); (3) LLM picks among ONLY the applicable subset (move_id + bounded subject); (4) deterministic template render. A pick OUTSIDE the applicable set is dropped at render (render keys on the applicable registry), so the LLM can never select outside the applicable set.
- [F1 f32626 'unchanged' baseline] DECIDED: compared to the pre-extraction rendered coaching — the existing offline workflow test (test_plan_review_workflow e2e) asserts the coaching for the always-applicable plan-review moves is byte-identical pre/post; a kernel test adds an applicability-filtered selection case (a move with applies_when excluded when its tag is absent).
- [E1 feca9 / T4 f7901 old-code removal] ADDRESSED: a re-export-identity test asserts passes.render_coach_notes / _validate_subject / coach_instructions ARE the kernel objects (no second copy); MOVE_REGISTRY stays as plan-review's catalog INSTANCE.
- [P6 f607d AC-quality / T7 f34d3 / T5e fea70 docs] DEFERRED docs to WS4 (epic seam+doc deliverable); WS3 adds kernel coach module docstrings recording the coach() contract + move-registry schema.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

Pass-4 coaching splits cleanly: the MECHANISM (map surviving advisories → a move from a locked
registry → deterministic template render; the LLM only picks `move_id` + a bounded noun-phrase
`subject`; no free prose; skip the LLM call when surviving==0) is generic; the move CATALOG
(plan-review's planning moves — spike, pre-mortem, ADR, thin-vertical-slice…) is domain-specific
(code review needs code moves). So the kernel should own the mechanism + a pluggable registry
SCHEMA, and each gate supplies its own catalog. This is also the right place to close the gap a
prior self-review flagged on the old epic: the registry is `{id, name, template}` with NO
applicability field, so move selection is wholly LLM-delegated — add a `triggers`/applies_when
field + a deterministic applicability filter.

## What

- Move the Pass-4 coach mechanism into the kernel: `coach(surviving, registry) -> notes` — the
  applicability filter → LLM picks among applicable moves (move_id + bounded subject) →
  deterministic render from the move template → the subject validator → the gate-on-surviving>0
  behavior.
- Define a registered move-registry SCHEMA `{id, name, template, triggers/applies_when}`; validate
  registries at load. Selection = deterministic filter on `triggers` THEN LLM choice among the
  applicable (not free LLM choice over the whole catalog).
- Make plan-review's existing move catalog a registry INSTANCE the kernel consumes; rewire the
  plan-review coach to call the kernel. No behavior change for the existing moves (their
  `applies_when` defaults to always-applicable unless they already had a stated trigger).

## Scope

In scope: the coach mechanism + subject validator + render + surviving>0 gate, moved to the
kernel; the move-registry schema with the applicability field; plan-review's catalog becomes an
instance. Out of scope: authoring a code-review move catalog (that is `b744`), Pass-2/3 (WS1/WS2),
the seam doc + evals (WS4). Touches `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/passes.py` (coach +
MOVE_REGISTRY + `_validate_subject`), `workflow_ops.py` (coach ops), the kernel module, and the
move-registry schema.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] The kernel exposes a Pass-4 `coach(surviving, registry)` that selects moves, renders
      deterministically from the template, validates the subject, and makes no LLM call when there
      are 0 surviving advisories.
- [ ] A registered move-registry schema includes a `triggers`/applies_when field; move selection
      applies a deterministic applicability filter before the LLM picks among applicable moves; a
      test covers an applicability-filtered selection.
- [ ] Plan-review's move catalog is a registry instance consumed by the kernel; an offline test
      asserts the rendered coaching is unchanged for the existing (always-applicable) moves.
- [ ] `make lint` + `make check` + the full test suite pass.
compiled_state.title: WS3: Extract Pass-4 coach mechanism + pluggable move-registry (triggers/applicability)
